checks.finding: No issues found. Every technology/architecture choice in design.md Components (lines 43-56) and Constraint Compliance (lines 152-161) ties to a stated rationale — e.g. cross-model-verify is 'existing sdd-quality-loop skill' reused 'without modification' (Assumptions, line 165-166), hook guard extension is 'same class as tasks.md Approval guard' (security-spec.md line 27), and domain-contract.json follows 'same pattern as design-tokens.json vs design-system.md' (design.md line 103). No bare unjustified 'we will use X' statements found (grep for will-use/selected/chosen patterns returned no matches).

No issues found. design.md ## Open Questions (lines 171-192) contains OQ-001 and OQ-002, each with Owner: human (a real role, not TBD), an explicit 'Blocks Implementation: no' field, and a concrete Resolution Path ('confirm at task approval; revisit after first retrospective with drift metrics' for OQ-001; 'confirm at task approval; revisit via WFI' for OQ-002). Both fields required by the check are present for both entries.

investigation.md absent (specs/sdd-domain/ directory listing confirms no investigation.md file) and design.md ## Assumptions (lines 163-169) contains 2 non-trivial entries with no empirical grounding: (1) 'cross-model-verify accepts a domain-artifact input bundle without skill changes' (line 165-166) — an external-behavior claim about an existing skill's compatibility, and (2) 'Existing c4-container template is reusable for stage 7 output' (line 167) — an external-artifact reusability claim. Neither cites an INV-xxx reference (none exist) nor a stated technical-default basis nor an explicit human-accepted-risk marker. Per check rule, this is a Major finding: run investigate-codebase or add investigation.md, or convert these into stated risks/defaults with an explicit basis.

No issues found. design.md ## Constraint Compliance (lines 152-161) is present and addresses all constraints identified in requirements.md: Visibility contract (AC-001/AC-011), Unidirectional flow, Graceful degradation (AC-010), Warn-first gates (AC-009), R-10 protected files (R-1), and Human-owned judgment. Additional explicit requirements.md constraints found via keyword scan — 'at most 3 rounds' (requirements.md line 90) and 'byte-identical' (lines 112, 128) — are addressed in design.md Components ('domain-review-loop... ≤3 rounds', line 48) and Test Strategy ('absence regression (byte-identical outputs without domain/)' and 'upstream byte-identical' for update-mode, lines 127-130).

No performance requirements found in requirements.md (keyword scan for response time/throughput/concurrency/latency/SLA/numeric-ms thresholds returned only a qualitative risk note, R-2: 'Cross-model verification at the domain gate adds cost/latency per model revision', which is a risk, not a numeric SLA). This qualitative concern is nonetheless addressed in design.md Risks ('run once per review-loop PASS, not per round', line 198-199) and infra-spec.md Cost Estimate/SLO tables.

No issues found. design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan (lines 145-150) states the deployment target ('No deployment... existing GitHub Actions validate job') and CI pipeline changes ('tests/validate-repository.ps1 with updated expectations plus the new Pester suites'). No schema changes exist (Data Plan: 'Migration Strategy: none required'), so migration-execution-order is not applicable. Incremental-rollout / feature-flag strategy is functionally the domain/ presence-absence gate, explicitly documented in Constraint Compliance ('Graceful degradation... checks domain/ existence first', line 158) and AC-010, which substitutes for an explicit feature-flag section for this opt-in-by-directory-presence feature.

design.md ## Data Plan (lines 89-105) states 'Existing Data Affected: none' and 'Migration Strategy: none required. Projects adopt by running /sdd-domain:domain-model; absence keeps legacy behavior (AC-010).' This matches the check's 'No data changes' PASS condition; no schema/column/table changes are declared.

No issues found. The one identifiable external/third-party integration — the GPT/Gemini cross-model panel — is named with purpose ('Blind multi-vendor verdict on the reviewed model', Components line 55; 'GPT/Gemini blind panel, reused', Architecture line 25), contract reference ('cross-model-verdict/v1 unchanged', API/Contract Plan line 116), and failure behaviour ('unavailable panelists fail toward requires_human_decision', Risks line 199; AC-017 and security-spec.md STRIDE row for B4 with TEST-006 evidence). It is explicitly reused, unmodified infrastructure from sdd-quality-loop, so vendor-level API/SDK versioning is owned by that existing skill, not redefined here. Seed-URL retrieval (issue URLs) is a generic harness-native read-only fetch, not a fixed third-party service, and is scoped in security-spec.md B1 with content-as-data and read-only-retrieval controls.

No issues found. Every design.md component maps to a requirements.md Goal or User Story: domain-model/domain-interviewer to US-1, domain-review-loop and cross-model-verify reuse to US-2, domain-sync and check-domain-conformance to US-3, domain-reverse to US-4. No component exceeds requirements Non-goals: design's pipeline stops at 'C4 Container' (matching the Non-goals line 56 'C4 Component/Code levels (Container level only in this lane)') and includes no CQRS/Event Sourcing implementation support or lite-track integration (matching Non-goals lines 54-55). No Goals or User Stories are left uncovered by design components.

No issues found. High-risk surfaces present (approval/authorization guard, PII/confidentiality in the cross-model bundle, cross-service integration failure behaviour, contract-integrity tampering) each name a concrete validation artifact: TEST-007/TEST-014 (tests/hooks/domain-approval-guard.Tests.ps1) for the approval guard; the bundle-exclusion rule plus TEST-006 (tests/sdd-domain/cross-model-gate.Tests.ps1) for PII/confidentiality and panelist-unavailable/mismatch failure behaviour (also AC-017); TEST-003/TEST-009/TEST-010 for domain-contract.json tampering and corruption handling. All are named test files or fixtures in Test Strategy and security-spec.md Security Tests table, not live-execution requirements.
